jenkins - kubernetes
====================================

Install kubectl in Jenkins node:

	refer: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/install-kubectl-linux/

#1. Download kubectl file from:
curl -LO "https://dl.k8s.io/release/$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl"
chmod +x kubectl
sudo mv kubectl /usr/local/bin
kubectl version
kubectl version --client
kubectl version --client --output=yaml


Update cluster config file in Jenkins Server:

Execute following command on control plane:

cat .kube/config

Output:

ubuntu@control-plane:~$ cat .kube/config
apiVersion: v1
clusters:
- cluster:
    certificate-authority-data: 
    server: https://172.31.42.136:6443
  name: kubernetes
contexts:
- context:
    cluster: kubernetes
    user: kubernetes-admin
  name: kubernetes-admin@kubernetes
current-context: kubernetes-admin@kubernetes
kind: Config
users:
- name: kubernetes-admin
  user:
    client-certificate-data: 
    client-key-data: 
ubuntu@control-plane:~$


in jenkins slave:
create a folder in ubuntu home dir:
mkdir .kube
cd .kube

ubuntu@node-1:~/.kube$ pwd
/home/ubuntu/.kube
ubuntu@node-1:~/.kube$

touch config

vi config

Add the config file details from kubernetes to jenkins worker node.

now in jenkins node, access kubectl commands:

kubectl get nodes


=====================
final jenkins cicd script:


pipeline {
    agent any

    stages {
        stage('Git-Clone') {
            steps {
                git 'https://github.com/devsecopstrainer/idream-microservice.git'
            }
        }
        stage('Mvn-Pkg') {
            steps {
                sh 'mvn clean package'
            }
        }
        stage('Docker-Build') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker build -t devsecopstrainer/spring-boot-app:latest .'
            }
        }
        stage('Push-Img-To-Docker-Hub') {
            steps {
                sh 'docker push devsecopstrainer/spring-boot-app:latest'
            }
        }
        stage('Deploy-On-K8S') {
            steps {
                sh 'kubectl apply -f deployment-ms.yml'
                sh 'kubectl apply -f svc-ms.yml'
            }
        }
    }
}
